confidence.text: Live dashboard currently uses reliable GHL conversation ownership/unread pressure plus recent Retell call activity. It is manager-useful now, but not yet full rep scorecard coverage.

repCards.name: Derek
repCards.needsAttentionReason: Queue looks relatively clean in the current sample.

managerActions: Pull the top unread inbound queues first. Those are the cleanest manager interventions.
Coach the rep with the highest stale conversation count before pushing more new leads.
Use the Retell feed to spot recent no-connect / voicemail patterns and tighten callback handling.
Treat unanswered inbound conversations as the priority leak before more reporting vanity work.

9.9% 2.25 points on all of these 12 month term

Hi Dave,

 

I wanted to follow up and start putting together some estimated DSCR refinance scenarios so you can get a clear picture of your
options and begin shaping your exit strategy.

 

Since you're considering testing the market with a potential sale, the goal here is to help you identify:

 * What price points make sense
 * Where your DSCR ratios will land
 * The minimum value you’d need to achieve to refinance without bringing cash to closing

 

To dial this in accurately, I’ll need a few key details from you:

 

Property Details

 * Full property address
 * Bed / bath count
 * Square footage (GLA)
 * Lot size

 

Income

 * Estimated market rent (what you believe the property would lease for today)

 

Expense Information

 * Property taxes (annual)
 * Insurance (annual)
 * HOA dues (if applicable)

 

Loan Details

 * Current payoff balance
 * Loan maturity date (if applicable)

 

Value & Rehab

 * Estimated current value (your best guess is fine)
 * Total amount invested into the rehab of the property

 

Once I have this, I’ll run a few scenarios to show you:

 * Estimated refinance proceeds at different valuation points
 * DSCR ratios across those scenarios
 * Where the “break-even” value sits for a no-cash-in refinance

 

That way, if you do decide to list the property, you’ll go in with a clear understanding of your thresholds and what outcomes
support your overall strategy.

 

Also, I wanted to introduce you to Brendon Garrett, who will be your primary point of contact moving forward and will be taking
over your account. Brendon is fully up to speed and will be working closely with you throughout this process.

 

That said, I’ll remain actively involved on this transaction and will continue to have visibility as we move forward.

 

Let me know if you want to talk through any of this—happy to help however you need.

 

Best,



Deric Gwinn

Co-Founder/Vice President

Phone: 623-693-5772

Email: deric@futuresfundinginc.com [deric@futuresfundinginc.com]

Web: www.futuresfundinginc.com [http://www.futuresfundinginc.com/]

[cid:image001.jpg@01DCC131.272E5B60]

Thanks Brendon! Ryan - can you let me know when this one is set up in the portal?
